etymology.description: 更(gēng, "to change") + 生(shēng, "to be born") = rebirth
etymology.interpretation: 甦(sū, "revive") combines 更(gēng, "to change") and 生(shēng, "to be born") to conceptually illustrate the idea of rebirth or being revived. The character communicates the notion of a transformation towards new life.

examples.chinese: 春天的清风让大地甦醒(春天的清風讓大地甦醒)。
examples.pinyin: Chūntiān de qīngfēng ràng dàdì sūxǐng.
examples.english: The spring breeze revives the earth.

examples.chinese: 经过雨水的滋润，花园里的植物开始甦醒(經過雨水的滋潤，花園裡的植物開始甦醒)。
examples.pinyin: Jīngguò yǔshuǐ de zīrùn，huāyuán lǐ de zhíwù kāishǐ sūxǐng.
examples.english: After being nourished by the rain, the plants in the garden begin to revive.

examples.chinese: 医护人员全力抢救，使病人得以甦醒(醫護人員全力搶救，使病人得以甦醒)。
examples.pinyin: Yīhù rényuán quánlì qiǎngjiù, shǐ bìngrén déyǐ sūxǐng.
examples.english: The medical staff made every effort to revive the patient.
